title: Search Indexing Application
description: This schema defines configuration for Search Reindexing Application.

batchSize.title: Batch Size
batchSize.description: Maximum number of events entities in a batch (Default 100).

payLoadSize.title: Payload Size
payLoadSize.description: Maximum number of events entities in a batch (Default 100).

producerThreads.title: Number of Producer Threads
producerThreads.description: Number of threads to use for reindexing

consumerThreads.title: Number of Consumer Threads
consumerThreads.description: Number of threads to use for reindexing

queueSize.title: Queue Size to use.
queueSize.description: Queue Size to use internally for reindexing.

maxConcurrentRequests.title: Max Concurrent Requests
maxConcurrentRequests.description: Maximum number of concurrent requests to the search index

maxRetries.title: Max Retries
maxRetries.description: Maximum number of retries for a failed request

entities.title: Entities
entities.description: List of entities that you need to reindex

autoTune.title: Auto Tune
autoTune.description: Enable automatic performance tuning based on cluster capabilities and database entity count

useDistributedIndexing.title: Use Distributed Indexing
useDistributedIndexing.description: Enable distributed indexing to scale reindexing across multiple servers with fault tolerance and parallel processing

partitionSize.title: Partition Size
partitionSize.description: Number of entities per partition for distributed indexing. Smaller values create more partitions for better distribution across servers. Range: 1000-50000.

timeSeriesMaxDays.title: Time Series Max Days
timeSeriesMaxDays.description: Maximum age in days for time series data during reindexing. Default 0 (index all data). Set to a positive value like 15 to limit to recent data only.

liveIndexSettings.title: Live Index Settings
liveIndexSettings.description: Settings applied to staged indexes before alias swap (live serving values). Tune for read freshness and HA. Defaults preserve current near-real-time read-after-write behavior. number_of_shards is omitted — it can only be set at index creation time, not via PUT _settings, and the staged-index reindex flow uses the static mapping JSON for creation.

numberOfReplicas.title: Number of Replicas
numberOfReplicas.description: Replica shard count. 1 for HA on multi-node clusters; 0 for single-node.

refreshInterval.title: Refresh Interval
refreshInterval.description: How often new writes become searchable. '1s' = near-real-time (required if users/agents read-after-write). Higher values reduce CPU/segment churn but delay search visibility.

translogDurability.title: Translog Durability
translogDurability.description: 'request' = fsync per write (durable). 'async' = fsync on interval (faster, can lose <syncInterval seconds on crash).

translogSyncInterval.title: Translog Sync Interval
translogSyncInterval.description: Translog fsync cadence when durability=async. Ignored when durability=request.

bulkIndexSettings.title: Bulk Index Settings (during reindex)
bulkIndexSettings.description: Overrides applied to staged indexes during bulk reindex for write throughput. Reverted to liveIndexSettings before alias swap. Nothing reads from staged indexes, so refresh=-1 and replicas=0 are safe.

numberOfReplicas.title: Number of Replicas
numberOfReplicas.description: Replica count during bulk write. 0 disables replica indexing for max throughput; replicas are populated after promotion from the new primary.

refreshInterval.title: Refresh Interval
refreshInterval.description: '-1' disables refresh during bulk write — no segment churn, dramatically faster. Staged index isn't searchable, so this is safe. Reverted to live value before swap.

translogDurability.title: Translog Durability
translogDurability.description: 'async' for bulk: fsync is batched, not per-write. Acceptable because a crashed reindex is rerunnable.

translogSyncInterval.title: Translog Sync Interval
translogSyncInterval.description: Relaxed translog fsync cadence during bulk. Ignored when durability=request.

forceMergeOnPromote.title: Force-merge before alias swap
forceMergeOnPromote.description: Run _forcemerge to 1 segment before swapping the alias. Improves post-reindex query performance at the cost of build time. Enable for large indexes serving heavy read traffic.
